_comment: Fixture fallback deck for the @kitsoki GitHub-loop demo. This is NOT the final live GitHub App POC deck: Act 1 uses the static gh-thread.html fixture and Act 2 uses no-LLM kitsoki web replay. Keep it for deterministic regression/storyboard review only. The live POC deck must be built from real bsacrobatix/Kitsoki issues/PRs, real webhook deliveries to kitsoki-test, real App comments, and real /run/<job-id> pages. Render: node /Users/brad/code/slidey/src/index.js kitsoki-github.deck.json <out>.mp4 → narrated MP4 + .chapters.json sidecar.

personas.name: Mara
personas.intro: Mentions @kitsoki in the fixture storyboard and reviews the expected response shape.

personas.name: kitsoki
personas.intro: Shows the expected mention, dispatch, status, and run-link behavior. The live proof comes from the GitHub App POC deck, not this fixture.

scenes.title: @kitsoki — GitHub agent storyboard
scenes.subtitle: Deterministic fallback footage; not live GitHub App evidence
scenes.narration: This is the fixture fallback for the @ kitsoki GitHub loop. It is useful for regression and storyboard review, but it is not the live GitHub App proof.

scenes.title: The cast
scenes.caption: Two actors, one thread — every hand-off a comment, every run a linkable, replayable record.
scenes.narration: Two actors share the storyboard thread: a maintainer who mentions kitsoki, and kitsoki, represented by fixture footage. The live POC must replace this act with real GitHub App evidence.

scenes.title: Who does what

cases.action: Mentions @kitsoki on a labelled issue or PR
Reads the label and dispatches the right story

cases.detail: not claimed as live POC behavior

cases.action: Opens the run link into the kitsoki viewer
Drives the run from the composer

scenes.narration: Across the storyboard, the maintainer mentions kitsoki and later opens the run shape. The live POC currently proves mention ingress, story dispatch, comments, and run pages; full PR autopilot remains future work.

scenes.title: GitHub-side storyboard
scenes.subtitle: Static fixture footage for deterministic regression
scenes.narration: First, the GitHub-side storyboard. This static fixture must not be used as the primary live POC evidence.

scenes.title: On the thread

cases.detail: one mention, one job, one link

cases.action: Routes a feature issue to the design track

scenes.caption: Eight fixture beats on a single thread — expected shapes only.
scenes.narration: On the fixture thread, a bug-labelled mention is the ingress. kitsoki acks with one run link and routes by label. The live POC deck must replace this with real GitHub issue, webhook, App comment, and run-page evidence.

scenes.title: Mention → dispatch storyboard
scenes.caption: Static GitHub-looking fixture, retained for regression only
scenes.narration: Here the fixture plays out the expected thread shape: mention, ack, dispatch, guidance, and rolling status. It is not live GitHub App proof.

scenes.title: The kitsoki-side replay
scenes.subtitle: No-LLM replay footage for UI shape
scenes.narration: Now, the kitsoki-side replay. It shows UI shape under deterministic replay, not a hosted live GitHub job.

scenes.title: In the viewer

cases.action: Opens the run link into the observer
Lands each turn; the state badge advances

scenes.caption: Replay viewer shape — not the hosted live run page.
scenes.narration: The maintainer opens the replay viewer shape and watches the trace stream. This deterministic footage remains useful, but the live POC deck must show the hosted run page at kitsoki-test.

scenes.title: Replay trace → operator surface
scenes.caption: Real kitsoki web in no-LLM replay; not a live GitHub job.
scenes.narration: This is a real kitsoki web server in no-LLM replay: the observer streams a replay trace and the operator surface advances. It is not the same job as a live GitHub thread.

scenes.title: Storyboard before live proof
scenes.subtitle: Replace with real GitHub App evidence for the final POC deck
scenes.narration: This fallback deck records the intended shape. The final POC deck must use real GitHub App webhook evidence, real App comments, and real hosted run pages.
Use this fallback for deterministic regression only. The polished GitHub act must come from live captured evidence.
scenes.tagline: Fixture fallback — live GitHub App proof still required
scenes.url: use live evidence for the final deck
